theme: Claude-Code style model-driven CAD Agent V2

items.title: Update canonical docs to V2 contract
Add LLM tool-calling interfaces and provider support
Implement tool orchestration, hooks, and turn state
Implement turn-grouped context manager and compact budgets
Refactor runner into adapter with legacy/v2 dispatch
Split validate_requirement feedback into core and diagnostic lanes
Shrink registry and prompt-facing policy exposure for V2
Slim V2 prompt surface to status-first and tool-partition-first context
Keep V2 prompt compaction JSON-safe when write payloads contain binary artifacts
Refresh benchmark naming, aggregation, case analysis, and trace usefulness for V2
Emit Claude-Code-style public decision logs, conversation trace, tool timeline, stop reason, and failure bundle
Rerun sampled L1 on V2 after benchmark diagnostics refresh and classify dominant failure modes
Run sampled L2 10-case benchmark after major slices
Run at least one real V2 probe under test_runs
Make execute_cadquery persist authoritative session state and support post-code validation/query/render
Allow code-path validation to fall back from sketch history to topology, face geometry, and conservative geometry summary
Rerun full L1 after execute_cadquery session bridge and validator fallback, then repair remaining failures only from completed evidence
Replace synthetic assistant replay with attachment-style transcript plus objective health, and normalize face aliases in tool/service layer
Keep raw validate_requirement contract stable but derive loop-safe core blockers inside V2 runtime so provenance-only blockers do not dominate stop/continue
Respect explicit timeout settings for kimi-k2.5-thinking and remove the old 300s floor behavior
Use final snapshot topology/geometry to validate code-path face edits and positioned hole alignment when structured action history is absent
Use final snapshot face structure to validate same-shape hollow frames, triangle pocket alignment, and targeted fillets when execute_cadquery bypasses structured history
Inject compact CAD runtime skill notes for reusable repair families instead of adding runner-local patches
Detect flat zero-volume nominal solids and teach the model to repair axisymmetric parts with coaxial primitives instead of retrying the same revolve family
Rerun L1 failure clusters after timeout/validator/skill-layer fixes and keep iterating from completed benchmark evidence
Add generic runtime skill guidance that maps named-plane symmetric profile extrudes to global box dimensions for whole-part execute_cadquery rebuilds
Probe the first successful session-backed execute_cadquery write immediately so whole-part code paths can stop before drifting into redundant inspection or repairs
Promote named-plane positive-extrude span from a soft runtime skill to a loop-safe core validation fact so centered whole-part rewrites cannot be marked complete by mistake
Make query_sketch expose the current sketch window first and keep only minimal path ancestry for path-attached sketches
After successful execute_cadquery whole-part rewrites, refresh validation once before another broad inspection turn when blockers are stale or rounds are exhausted
Expose round budget and current sketch completion risk so the model can choose whole-part rewrites under tight budgets
Persist failed write-tool results, surface previous_tool_failure_summary, and keep last good evidence available for repair turns
Recognize circular profile shape and notch/slot subtractive intent from execute_cadquery final snapshots when structured local history is absent
Mark the run as converged whenever benchmark-finalization validation proves completion
Stop the V2 loop immediately when non-progress auto-validation returns complete instead of exhausting rounds waiting for finish_run
Recognize explicit multi-plane additive unions from execute_cadquery final snapshots when datum-plane extrude specs imply a unique bbox/volume signature
Recognize mixed nested inner-void sections and axisymmetric annular groove windows from execute_cadquery final snapshots when structured history is absent
Reject unsupported additive-extrude semantic overloads instead of silently degrading them into plain extrudes
